questions.question: Which of the following condition is NOT one of the four Coffman conditions required for a deadlock to occur?
What hardware component translates virtual addresses to physical addresses in a virtual memory system?
What is the phenomenon called when an operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of memory than executing actual instructions?
Which scheduling algorithm can potentially lead to the starvation of long-running processes?
What is a 'race condition' in concurrent programming?
answers: When a program runs out of memory
When output depends on the non-deterministic sequence or timing of execution threads
questions.correctAnswer: When output depends on the non-deterministic sequence or timing of execution threads

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To speed up network data transfers

questions.question: Which type of interrupt is triggered directly by an executing instruction, such as a division by zero?
What is the main advantage of using a microkernel architecture compared to a monolithic kernel?
answers: Direct hardware access for user applications

questions.question: In Unix-like systems, what system call is used to create a completely new child process that is a duplicate of the parent?
Which RAID configuration uses disk striping with distributed parity, requiring at least three drives?
